examples: Todd Lapin founded Telstar Logistics, a fictional company whose name and mark is used to gain access to places most of us couldn't reach.
TELSTAR functions as a crucial supporting system for telecom operation and maintenance sectors during management and production activities.
Telstar makes real a 1945 concept by science fiction author Arthur C. Clarke, who envisioned a global communications network based on geosynchronous satellites.
Mr Pierce's Telstar, launched two years later, carried television pictures between America and Europe as well as phone calls.
Amazingly, after all that, Telstar is still up there, orbiting the Earth, a sleeping relic of a bygone age.
